gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that validation-summary records were skipped by record_type alone, then found an ok:true empty-files summary could still be skipped. The validator now skips only valid non-empty validation summaries whose nested PR evidence records evaluate cleanly; invalid summaries fall through as failed sidecars. Final GPT-5.5 review found no remaining issues.
verification: find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
what_changed: Made evaluate_pr_evidence_directory recursively discover PR evidence sidecars by default.
Skipped only structurally valid, ok:true PR evidence validation summary records, while malformed or failed summaries remain validation failures.
Added regression tests for nested PR evidence sidecars, explicit recursive=False behavior, valid validation-summary skipping, invalid validation summaries, and empty ok:true summaries.
Documented recursive directory validation behavior in pr-evidence/README.md.
why: New PR slices store evidence in per-slice subdirectories, so the repository governance gate must validate nested traceability sidecars instead of only top-level pr-evidence JSON files.
